question: Which metric best reflects automation suite reliability over time?

Code line count
Number of endpoints

question: When monitoring Selenium Grid performance, what indicates Node saturation?

correct_answer: The error details in the JSON response body

question: You run 'curl -v https://api.internal.company.com' and get 'SSL: certificate subject name does not match target host name'. The IP resolves correctly. What is the issue?

The CA bundle is missing on the client
The server is using HTTP/2 which is incompatible in this scenario under production constraints for the stated requirement
The SSL certificate is expired
correct_answer: The certificate's Subject Alternative Name (SAN) or Common Name (CN) does not include 'api.internal.company.com'

question: What is the difference between a hard link and a soft (symbolic) link in Linux?
options: A hard link is a directory entry pointing to the same inode (same data blocks); a soft link is a special file containing the path to the target - it breaks if the target is moved/deleted, and works across filesystems

Soft links share the same inode as the target; hard links create a copy in this scenario under production constraints for the stated requirement during incident triage for production reliability and controlled rollback
Hard links work across filesystems; soft links do not
There is no practical difference between them
correct_answer: A hard link is a directory entry pointing to the same inode (same data blocks); a soft link is a special file containing the path to the target - it breaks if the target is moved/deleted, and works across filesystems

question: In what scenario would you use Robot class instead of Actions class in Selenium?

Robot cannot interact with web elements under production constraints in enterprise environments
Robot is always preferred
Robot is deprecated
correct_answer: For OS-level interactions like keyboard shortcuts, file uploads outside browser

question: During live operations, which option best describes the purpose of Linux kernel parameter 'net.ipv4.ip_forward=1'?
options: It enables IP packet forwarding between network interfaces, turning the system into a router; required for NAT, VPN gateways, and container networking
It forwards all traffic to localhost
It enables the system to forward ICMP echo requests only in this scenario under production constraints for the stated requirement during incident triage
It enables IPv6 compatibility mode
correct_answer: It enables IP packet forwarding between network interfaces, turning the system into a router; required for NAT, VPN gateways, and container networking

question: A production server is experiencing high load averages (15+) but CPU utilization stays under 20%. 'top' shows many processes in D (uninterruptible sleep) state. What is the most likely root cause?
options: Processes are waiting on I/O operations (disk or NFS), causing I/O wait
The system is CPU-bound and needs more cores
The scheduler is misconfigured and needs tuning via nice values in this scenario
OOM killer is throttling processes
correct_answer: Processes are waiting on I/O operations (disk or NFS), causing I/O wait
